# Meter

Gauge semantics with automatic good/warn/critical coloring driven by the low/high/optimum attributes — the browser picks the state, CSS picks the colors.

```html
<div class="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-3 text-sm">
  <label class="grid gap-1.5">
    <span class="flex justify-between font-medium">Storage <span class="font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground">28%</span></span>
    <meter
      value="0.28"
      low="0.5"
      high="0.8"
      optimum="0.2"
      class="h-2 w-full appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:h-2 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:border-0 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-secondary [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-emerald-500 [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-amber-500 [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-destructive [&::-moz-meter-bar]:rounded-full"
    ></meter>
  </label>
  <label class="grid gap-1.5">
    <span class="flex justify-between font-medium">Memory <span class="font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground">67%</span></span>
    <meter
      value="0.67"
      low="0.5"
      high="0.8"
      optimum="0.2"
      class="h-2 w-full appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:h-2 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:border-0 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-secondary [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-emerald-500 [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-amber-500 [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-destructive [&::-moz-meter-bar]:rounded-full"
    ></meter>
  </label>
  <label class="grid gap-1.5">
    <span class="flex justify-between font-medium">CPU <span class="font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground">91%</span></span>
    <meter
      value="0.91"
      low="0.5"
      high="0.8"
      optimum="0.2"
      class="h-2 w-full appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:h-2 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:appearance-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:border-0 [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-bar]:bg-secondary [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-optimum-value]:bg-emerald-500 [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value]:bg-amber-500 [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:rounded-full [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-none [&::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value]:bg-destructive [&::-moz-meter-bar]:rounded-full"
    ></meter>
  </label>
</div>
```

## How it works

- `low` / `high` / `optimum` tell the browser which ranges are good; it then exposes one of
  three pseudo-elements — all sharing the `::-webkit-meter-` prefix: `-optimum-value`,
  `-suboptimum-value`, or `-even-less-good-value` — and each gets its own color utility. The state logic ships with
  the browser.
- The native bar ships a glossy vertical gradient as a `background-image`, so a
  `background-color` alone can't flatten it — set `bg-none` first to reset `background-image`,
  then your `bg-*` color lands flat.
- Firefox only exposes `::-moz-meter-bar`, so it keeps its native state colors under your
  radius — an honest fallback.
- Fair warning: meter is the platform's dustiest corner. Keep styling modest (height, radius,
  colors) and it behaves; fight it and Safari wins.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

None. This component uses only Baseline CSS/HTML — it works the same in every current browser.
